response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins by releasing histamines which can cause severe allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis which can be life-threatening if not treated immediately  
the presence of water does not neutralize or remove the peanut proteins from the environment which means exposure to even trace amounts can trigger a reaction in sensitive individuals  

Sec. 2  
living underwater would require a person to be in constant contact with water which may contain contaminants or substances that could interact with the body in unpredictable ways  
furthermore the physical and physiological demands of underwater living such as pressure changes and oxygen supply are separate challenges that are unrelated to peanut allergies but still critical to human survival in such an environment
